combat_history.event: ROKS Gyeongbuk conducted extended ASW patrol operations following detection of suspected North Korean submarine activity near Yeonpyeong Island
combat_history.significance: First operational ASW mission for class, validated sonar performance and crew training

combat_history.event: Multiple Daegu-class frigates participated in territorial defense exercises around disputed Dokdo/Takeshima islands, conducting surface and air defense scenarios
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated multi-ship coordination capabilities and K-CMS networking

combat_history.event: ROKS Daegu conducted missile firing exercises with Haeseong anti-ship missiles, achieving successful target engagement at maximum range
combat_history.significance: Validated indigenous missile integration and long-range engagement capabilities

modernization.description: Upgrade to Korean Vertical Launch System to accommodate longer-range SAMs and potential land-attack missiles. Includes software updates for K-CMS integration.
modernization.impact: Extends air defense engagement envelope and provides limited land-attack capability

modernization.description: Installation of advanced electronic warfare systems developed for KDX-III Batch-II destroyers, including improved jamming and deception capabilities.
modernization.impact: Enhanced survivability against modern anti-ship missiles and improved electronic attack capabilities

modernization.description: Integration of unmanned surface vehicles (USVs) and enhanced helicopter drone operations for extended sensor coverage and mine countermeasures.
modernization.impact: Force multiplication for ASW and mine warfare missions

vulnerabilities.description: Only 16 VLS cells significantly limits sustained engagement capability compared to contemporary frigates with 32+ cells
vulnerabilities.context: Against saturation attacks or extended combat operations, magazine depth becomes critical limitation
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Emphasis on networking with other platforms and shore-based systems for distributed engagement

vulnerabilities.description: K-CMS and Korean sensor suite lack combat-proven track record compared to Western systems like Aegis or TACTICOS
vulnerabilities.context: Software bugs or integration issues could compromise combat effectiveness in high-threat scenarios
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Continuous software updates and extensive training exercises to identify issues

vulnerabilities.description: Design optimized for Korean Peninsula operations lacks fuel capacity and crew facilities for extended deployments
vulnerabilities.context: Restricts ability to participate in distant coalition operations or power projection missions
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Focus on regional role with potential for future variants with enhanced endurance

vulnerabilities.description: One helicopter hangar limits ASW coverage and creates single point of failure for rotary-wing operations
vulnerabilities.context: Modern submarine threats require persistent helicopter coverage for effective prosecution
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Coordination with shore-based maritime patrol aircraft and other surface units

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Regional patrol operations in Yellow Sea and East Sea, Northern Limit Line enforcement, ASW barrier operations
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Usually operates independently or in pairs, integrated with KDX-II/III destroyers for higher-threat scenarios
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High operational tempo due to North Korean threat maintains most units at high readiness; maintenance burden increasing as fleet ages

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Russian design emphasizes heavier missile armament (24 VLS cells) but lacks stealth features and has older sensors. Grigorovich has better sustained firepower but Daegu has superior radar signature management.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: East vs West frigate design philosophy - missile capacity vs stealth and sensors

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Chinese frigate has 32 VLS cells and better endurance but larger radar signature. Type 054A represents proven design while Daegu incorporates newer stealth technology but limited combat experience.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Korean Peninsula naval balance - indigenous Korean capability vs Chinese mass production

peer_comparisons.key_differences: FREMM offers superior sensors (EMPAR radar) and 16-32 VLS cells but costs significantly more. European design optimized for global operations vs Korean regional focus.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Cost vs capability - can indigenous Asian designs match European standards?

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Japanese design emphasizes ASW and uses proven Western systems (Aegis-derived) but lacks indigenous missile integration. Mogami has better sensors but Daegu has more diverse armament.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Allied approaches to regional frigate design - Japanese integration vs Korean indigenization

peer_comparisons.key_differences: German frigate has superior air defense radar (APAR) and 32 VLS cells but much higher cost and complexity. Represents high-end European approach vs Korean cost-effectiveness focus.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: European premium vs Asian value engineering in modern frigate design
